commands.command: pnpm -C packages/tractor-ts exec wasm-tools component wit ../../validations/astro-wasi-ssr/wit
commands.result: The local WIT world resolves after vendoring the official wasi:http, wasi:io, and wasi:clocks v0.2.3 subset used by incoming-handler.

commands.stderrIncludes: ReferenceError: Error loading module "node:module" (resolved path "dist/server/node:module"): No such file or directory
commands.result: ComponentizeJS resolves the local WIT world and starts evaluating the Astro server bundle, then fails on the bundle's Node built-in import surface. The package script remains bounded by a 45s timeout to avoid future hangs.
Static inspection of the generated Astro 7 server bundle shows Node-specific surfaces, starting with node:module in dist/server/index.mjs.

resolved.summary: The fixture vendors the minimal official WASI v0.2.3 WIT graph needed for wasi:http/incoming-handler: wasi:http types/handler, wasi:io error/poll/streams, and wasi:clocks monotonic-clock/wall-clock.

blocker.summary: After WIT resolution, ComponentizeJS/StarlingMonkey cannot initialize the generated Astro 7 server bundle because it imports Node-specific surfaces such as node:module. The bundle also references process, Buffer, and sharp, so Part C needs a WASI-oriented Astro bundle profile before host execution is meaningful.

secondaryRisk.summary: The componentize script is intentionally bounded by timeout 45s because ComponentizeJS/Wizer can hang on bundle/runtime drift and should not be allowed to pin the development container.

nextAction: Decide whether Part C should pursue a custom Astro WASI adapter or bundle profile that removes Node built-ins, starting with node:module/process/Buffer/sharp, or record Part C as red and keep ADR-070 Parts A/B.
